element_introduction_order.element: Basic car operation (manual transmission, clutch, shifter)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players must properly operate realistic transmission mechanics or risk stalling; difficulty increases with manual vs automatic selection
Simulated road course with timed completion (~90 seconds), establishes baseline speed and steering physics
Very short duration (~30 seconds) emphasizes acceleration and quick turns over endurance
Corkscrew loop, cylindrical tunnel, 45° inclined hill, dual landing ramps, mountain road sections; requires precise vehicle control and physics understanding

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Stunt track (original Hard Drivin' elements) and Super Stunt track (enhanced versions)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Dual landing ramps and jump loop sections demand timing and trajectory management

typical_relief_triggers: Crossing finish line within time limit
typical_tension_triggers: Risk of vehicle stall (manual transmission misshift)
Narrow track sections requiring precision steering
Jump ramps with landing accuracy requirements
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Time-trial based tension with discrete peaks at stunt sections; relief comes from obstacle completion or maintaining steady pace; overall arc is gradual escalation from short speed-focused Autocross (30s) to endurance-heavy Super Stunt (180s)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Autocross track provides brief, accessible introduction to vehicle physics and steering responsiveness; 30-second duration allows quick iteration
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Original Stunt track (~90 seconds) introduces standard track navigation with moderate obstacle complexity and time management demands
difficulty_curve.late_game: Super Stunt track (~180 seconds) escalates with enhanced and new stunt obstacles (corkscrew loop, cylindrical tunnel, inclined hill) requiring sustained precision and advanced physics mastery
notable_spikes: Transition from Autocross to original Stunt track triples time commitment and track complexity
Introduction of corkscrew loop and cylindrical tunnel sections in Super Stunt track
Manual transmission vehicle selection dramatically increases difficulty via stall risk

notable_level_patterns.description: Tracks escalate from minimalist speed (Autocross) to moderate obstacles (original Stunt) to elaborate stunt sequences (Super Stunt with loop, tunnel, hill, ramps). Each track isolates different skill requirements.
Autocross and Super Stunt tracks offer only one driving path per course, unlike original Hard Drivin' which had multiple routes. This enforces specific obstacle navigation rather than player choice.

Manual transmission mode with clutch, 4-speed shifter, and stall risk creates a skill ceiling independent of track design. Realistic vehicle modeling (4-wheel instead of 2-wheel simulation) directly impacts control feedback.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All tracks with manual transmission vehicle selection
